expectations.text: Response includes a visual directory tree structure showing the full workspace layout
expectations.evidence: Lines 7-85: Detailed tree with src/, tests/, docs/ and subdirectories. Standard project structure, not ICM workspace.

expectations.text: Response includes a CLAUDE.md template with a routing table
expectations.evidence: Lines 92-137: Has CLAUDE.md but it is a flat architecture overview with commands list, NOT a routing table with Go to / Read / Skills columns. No task routing.

expectations.text: Response includes CONTEXT.md content for at least 2 individual stages
expectations.evidence: No CONTEXT.md files at all. Uses .claude/rules/ and .claude/settings.json instead. No staged workspace concept.

expectations.text: Response mentions token budgets or context window efficiency
expectations.evidence: No mention of tokens, context window, or budgets. Section 'How This Helps Claude Maintain Context' (lines 243-268) discusses cognitive load but not token scoping.

expectations.text: Response includes Layer 3 reference files (_config/ or references/) separate from working directories
expectations.evidence: Has .claude/rules/typescript-api.md for conventions, but no dedicated _config/ or references/ layer separated from working directories.

expectations.text: Response distinguishes between stable reference material and per-run working artifacts
expectations.evidence: No factory/product or reference/artifact distinction. All files treated equally in standard project layout.
